columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: Locate the sentence in Section 2.4 "Outcome measures and statistical analysis" that states: "Toxicity at 4 years postrandomisation was similar, with 16% patients in each group reporting grade 3 or higher toxicity." The text reports "16% patients in each group reporting grade 3 or higher toxicity" at 4 years postrandomisation. It does not explicitly distinguish between "all-cause" and "treatment-related" adverse events. The count (N) is not provided.

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 5 | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: In Section 4 "DISCUSSION", locate the sentence: "...and no grade 4 or 5 toxicity." This statement refers to the "worst reported grade of toxicity" for patients in the SOC + AAP group (treatment arm) at 2 years after randomisation. The text explicitly states "no grade 4 or 5 toxicity" for a subset of patients at 2 years. While not explicitly stated as "treatment-related", "toxicity" in this context is generally understood to refer to adverse effects of the treatment.

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 5 | Control
columns.extraction_plan: In Section 4 "DISCUSSION", locate the sentence: "...and no grade 4 or 5 toxicity." This statement refers to the "worst reported grade of toxicity" for patients in the SOC-alone group (control arm) at 2 years after randomisation. The text explicitly states "no grade 4 or 5 toxicity" for a subset of patients at 2 years. While not explicitly stated as "treatment-related", "toxicity" in this context is generally understood to refer to adverse effects of the treatment.
